rm: Remove files or directories from the filesystem
pwd: Print the current working directory path
mv: Move or rename files and directories
touch: Create empty files or update file timestamps
less: View file contents one page at a time (scrollable)
more: View file contents one page at a time
head: Display the first lines of a file
tail: Display the last lines of a file
grep: Search for patterns in files using regular expressions
find: Search for files and directories in a directory hierarchy
locate: Find files by name using a pre-built database
which: Locate the executable file for a command
whereis: Locate the binary, source, and manual page for a command
echo: Print text or variable values to standard output
chgrp: Change the group ownership of a file
sudo: Execute a command as another user (typically root/superuser)
id: Print user and group identity information
who: Show who is currently logged in
w: Show who is logged in and what they are doing
last: Show the last logins of users
top: Display dynamic real-time view of running processes
htop: Interactive process viewer (enhanced version of top)
kill: Send a signal to a process (default: terminate)
pkill: Send signals to processes based on name and attributes
bg: Resume a suspended job in the background
fg: Bring a background job to the foreground
jobs: List active jobs in the current shell session
nohup: Run a command immune to hangups, output to a file
nice: Run a command with modified scheduling priority
renice: Change the priority of running processes
free: Display amount of free and used memory
hostname: Show or set the system hostname
uptime: Tell how long the system has been running
date: Display or set the system date and time
reset: Reset the terminal to initial state
exit: Exit the current shell or terminal session
logout: Log out from the current session
shutdown: Shut down or reboot the system
tar: Archive files (tape archive); create, extract, or list archives
gzip: Compress files using the gzip algorithm
zip: Package and compress files into a ZIP archive
unzip: Extract files from a ZIP archive
bzip2: Compress files using the bzip2 algorithm
xz: Compress files using the xz algorithm
7z: Compress and extract files in 7-Zip format
curl: Transfer data from or to a server using various protocols
ping: Send ICMP ECHO_REQUEST packets to network hosts
traceroute: Print the route packets take to a network host
ifconfig: Configure or display network interface parameters
ip: Show or manipulate routing, network devices, and tunnels
netstat: Print network connections, routing tables, and statistics
scp: Secure copy files between hosts over SSH
rsync: Fast, versatile file copying and synchronization tool
nmap: Network exploration tool and security/port scanner
iptables: Administration tool for IPv4 packet filtering and NAT
apt: Advanced Package Tool for Debian/Ubuntu-based systems
apt-get: Command-line interface for the APT package manager
dnf: Dandified YUM - package manager for Fedora/RHEL-based systems
yum: Yellowdog Updater Modified - legacy package manager for RPM-based systems
rpm: RPM Package Manager - low-level package tool
yay: Yet Another Yaourt - AUR helper for Arch Linux
paru: AUR helper and pacman wrapper for Arch Linux
systemctl: Control the systemd system and service manager
service: Run a System V init script
journalctl: Query and display messages from the systemd journal
at: Schedule a command to run once at a particular time
watch: Execute a program periodically and show output
xargs: Build and execute command lines from standard input
tee: Read from stdin and write to stdout and files simultaneously
wc: Print newline, word, and byte counts for each file
cut: Remove sections from each line of files
sed: Stream editor for filtering and transforming text
patch: Apply a diff file to an original
readlink: Print the resolved path of a symbolic link
stat: Display file or file system status
strings: Print printable character sequences found in a file
hexdump: Display file contents in hexadecimal or other formats
od: Dump files in octal and other formats
base64: Encode or decode data in Base64 format
md5sum: Compute and check MD5 message digest
sha256sum: Compute and check SHA256 message digest
openssl: OpenSSL command-line tool for cryptographic operations
gpg: GNU Privacy Guard - encryption and signing tool
mkfs: Build a Linux filesystem on a device or partition
fsck: Check and repair a Linux filesystem
dd: Convert and copy a file (low-level data copying)
dmesg: Print or control the kernel ring buffer
modprobe: Add or remove modules from the Linux kernel
lsmod: Show the status of modules in the Linux kernel
insmod: Insert a module into the Linux kernel
rmmod: Remove a module from the Linux kernel
docker: Container platform for building and running applications
kubectl: Command-line tool for Kubernetes cluster management
nano: Simple text editor for the terminal
tmux: Terminal multiplexer - manage multiple terminal sessions
export: Set environment variables for child processes
source: Execute commands from a file in the current shell
type: Describe how a name would be interpreted by the shell
man: Display the manual page for a command
help: Display help for shell built-in commands
apropos: Search the manual page names and descriptions
tput: Initialize a terminal or query terminfo database
stty: Change and print terminal line settings
lsof: List open files and the processes that opened them
fuser: Identify processes using files or sockets
pgrep: Look up processes based on name and attributes
pidof: Find the process ID of a running program
time: Measure the time taken to execute a command
timeout: Run a command with a time limit
yes: Output a string repeatedly until killed
factor: Print prime factors of given numbers
sleep: Delay for a specified amount of time
wait: Wait for a process to complete
read: Read a line from standard input
write: Send a message to another user
wall: Write a message to all logged-in users
mesg: Control write access to your terminal
ip6tables: Administration tool for IPv6 packet filtering
nft: Nftables - administration tool for packet filtering
whois: Client for the whois directory service
tracepath: Traces path to a network host discovering MTU along this path
mtr: Network diagnostic tool combining ping and traceroute
netcat: Utility for reading/writing data across network connections
nc: Utility for reading/writing data across network connections (alias for netcat)
socat: Multipurpose relay for bidirectional data transfer
cryptsetup: Manage plain dm-crypt and LUKS encrypted volumes
fstrim: Discard unused blocks on a mounted filesystem
smartctl: Control and monitor storage systems using SMART
badblocks: Search a device for bad blocks
tune2fs: Adjust tunable filesystem parameters on ext2/ext3/ext4
update-grub: Update GRUB bootloader on Debian-based systems
grub-install: Install GRUB bootloader to a device
modinfo: Show information about a Linux kernel module
ionice: Set or get process I/O scheduling class and priority
taskset: Set or retrieve a process CPU affinity
sar: Collect, report, and save system activity information
iostat: Report CPU statistics and I/O statistics for devices
iftop: Display bandwidth usage on an interface
auditctl: Control the behavior of the audit system
setenforce: Modify the mode SELinux is running in
restorecon: Restore file(s) default SELinux security contexts
aa-enforce: Set an AppArmor profile to enforce mode
apparmor_parser: Load AppArmor profiles into the kernel
